item.title: Why don't more people talk about this?
item.content:encoded:
This went from silly and hilarious to absolutely horrifying in a matter of minutes.

+1

Why the @+$# don't more people talk about this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for necro, but I have to agree, that sentence also made me stumble when I read the article. It just seems ham-fisted.

My suggestion would be something like:

"Several caretakers have noted that the resulting/displayed patterns often resemble moonlight reflecting off the sea."

item.title: Two things
item.content:encoded:
1. The 90-100 instances in the beginning vs. the 5000+ instances at the end is confusing to me. I saw this addressed in an earlier comment and that the intention is to suggest that things have gotten out of hand. It does not work for me, because I like to think the Foundation is very methodical about its procedures. They would have some sort of alert going of or some automated kill-switches or something. It's not really successfully contained otherwise, I'd say. And why would they even keep that many in the first place? Wouldn't 12 or 18 be enough?`

2. I don't get the eye of the storm part. I'm sure there is something I'm missing. I felt like there should be something happening to Dr. Carson other than "not being recovered". Like, he could merge with the lizzard mass and become some sort of grotesque lizard man. Or just survive because they somehow have sympathy for him - at least -4s actions make it seem like that…

otherwise, this is really entertaining and I think you did an amazing job hitting dat sailors language. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well. That escalated quickly.
I always love it when an SCP is written in such a way that, just as you think you understand it, it takes a hard turn and surprises you!
It was also nice to see blue-tongued lizards being given some love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one made me smile, so long as I was able to keep nautical stuff away from them I bet I'd like to talk with these SCPs. We could sing sea shanties… or would that trigger a breach?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: have an upboat you glorious addled feline

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Drowning in a swarm of singing lizards… thats… certainly one way to go….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: magnificently, tremendously weird. i adore this, toe to tip, from its delightful premise to its dark finish. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the Catholic forest creatures all over again, and not done as well. I felt there was nothing terribly exciting in the main body and there was a lot riding on the interview to carry it. This then turned out to be only all right. I was hoping for more of a twist than just "they keep multiplying!"

Not to my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks bud! I let someone else change it but I'll change it to yours later when I'm home!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one (admittedly trivial) nitpick concerns the Latin names - the skink in the picture is actually Tiliqua rugosa and not T. scincoides intermedia as the text claims. Otherwise, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The skinks have always been. The skinks will always be. Their mother is the mother in the sea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. :C It's off-kilter, but not quite enough. And little things like the quote marks in the interview and the preponderance of 'non-scientific terms in single quotes' really bug me. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this the one? It's by DrKens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: haha thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully silly, I love the idea of them swarming their way to containment breach, characterization is really good. I do think the interview lines in quotation marks is strangely off-putting, but I will let it slide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. There's no way I could've figured that one out on my own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAHHH! CUTE 682! BUT ITS NOT -CU?!? HOOOWWWW???

Well, that was cute.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is in turn a play on "Loose Lips Sink Ships", an American WWII Propaganda slogan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The title of the SCP is Loose Lips Skink ships. Its a play on Loose lips loose scips or something like that on a poster that a user made (That also appeared in CB)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't particularly moved until the interview, but I loved the way it went from cute and funny to scary in just a couple lines (I was wondering why it was Euclid until that point).

Also, anyone care to explain the pun? I definitely cannot see what they did there >__> (English is not my first language)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to look at it as documentation being in progress in-universe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I surmised that, but I'd still rather it be stated outright that they originally numbered ninety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its like, that is the original amount they had but the counter has basically said it has just gotten out of control since.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it… odd that the procedure starts by stating there are 90 of them and concludes by saying there are over three thousand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah bugger. Sorry Zyn, I though I had the tone concept down. Apologies, and I'll try to keep this in mind in the future. Thanks for the correction!

(edit: also I have tendency to capitalize random things when writing. Forgot to get rid of that.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the dancing skinks. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering I was trying to hit on a comedy rather than super duper serious science, I did not want to hit it too hard with all the science lingo. I wanted to get away with a semi-serious SCP. As some may know by now I am more of a comedy writer rather than a serious writer.

I have, however, fixed up some random caps that strayed in and random numbers that were wrong. Even after checking those numbers twice and thrice there was still just a couple that got away. Some grammar has also been fixed up.

item.title: Re: SCP-3011
item.content:encoded: Link has been replaced. Thanks dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seams a bit too fanceyful for the foundation. Ether put that description in quotes or rewrite it into something like:

Caretakers have observed that this in appearance is reminiscent of Moonlight reflecting of water, specific in reference to the ocean.

*seems *fanciful

Also, Dr0Shadow, the text that you recommend replacing and your replacement suggestion contain very much different information. Something resembling another thing, provided you take the first usual definition, can be interpreted as rather different from something being reminiscent of another thing (e.g., an enormous chocolate strawberry may be reminiscent of your mother if she made those for you, but hopefully you never tell your mother that she resembles an enormous chocolate strawberry)

You don't need to capitalize "moonlight" as it's not a proper noun, the correct word would be "specifically" since it refers to the verb "reflecting", and "in reference to the ocean" is overly wordy when what is currently there suffices for clinical tone, as it's stated that the reader is being told of the opinion of caretakers.

Clinical tone does not mean overcomplicating text to try and sound more professional. Concise, precise language is preferred over wordiness, even if said wordiness sounds more technical.

Please be more careful when attempting to critique clinical tone from now on.

item.title: Re: SCP-3011
item.content:encoded:
Accidentally gave Roth a non-complaint image, that one is NC.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_tongued_skink_NSW_Australia.JPG

this image however is compatible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show The Review
- OH GADS SHAGGY BASICALLY COVERED THESE POINTS ALREADY CLOSE IT

Caretakers have observed that this resembles the moon reflecting off the sea.

Seams a bit too fanciful for the foundation. —Ether put that description in quotes or rewrite it into something like:

Caretakers have observed that this in appearance is reminiscent of moonlight reflecting of water, specific in reference to the ocean.

Or something to that effect.— EDIT: Danke, Zyn.

Doctor Carson: "Where are you from?"

SCP-3011-1: "Oh, we're a long way from our home, my boy. Born on the ragin' sea, you see. We don't make to land, 'least not that we plan."

SCP-3011-2: "And now here we be."

Doctor Carson: Alright, but how did you get here?

[…]

The erratic quotation marks are too distracting for me to read it. I'd personally remove them all together, but fixing the quotation marks on all of them works too.

Besides those points here have a +1

P.S. OH HI ROTH DIDN'T SEE IT WAS YOU CONGRATS!

P.P.S. Oh I see what you did there.

+ spoilers
- hippity hop

Will we see a rabbit inspired skip?

item.title: Lies!
item.content:encoded:
There were loose lips (or, tattoos if we wanna be accurate), but no ships were sunk.

+1

EDIT: I can't believe I didn't see the pun until after posting this. +1, then -1 just so that I could give it a second +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thanks for that! I'll deff have a look over it soon and fix things!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's certainly fun and interesting! The interview log contains numerous errors, like an it's mistake, random capitalization, and wonky formatting. -1 due solely to that, but I'll definitely revisit for a +1 once it's cleaned up.

item.title: SCP-3011
item.content:encoded:
Written with much help and input from Roget, Vivax and Wogglebug, Drewbear and Quikngruvn! Voct has added their own little bit to the containment procedure.

Thanks to LilyFlower, Ihp and Wogglebug for critt!

Image from
Link to original image
LONG LIVE THE SKINKS!

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_tongued_skink_NSW_Australia.JPG

Mistakes such as random caps and numbers being wrong have been fixed. All "s have been removed.

Did you see what we did there?
